title: Association of Nausea and Vomiting During Pregnancy With Pregnancy Loss
abstractQuote: Nausea and vomiting were common very early in pregnancy and were associated with a reduced risk for pregnancy loss.
notes: Supports association between nausea and reduced miscarriage risk. Not predictive at individual level.

title: First trimester bleeding
abstractQuote: Vaginal bleeding in the first trimester occurs in about one fourth of pregnancies.
notes: Provides prevalence data for first-trimester bleeding.

title: The timing of the fertile window in the menstrual cycle
abstractQuote: In only about 30% of women is the fertile window entirely within days 10 to 17.
notes: Demonstrates variability of fertile window and challenges day-14 ovulation myth.

title: Effectiveness of lactational amenorrhoea in prevention of pregnancy in Manila
abstractQuote: The lactational amenorrhoea method was 99% effective when used correctly for up to six months.
notes: Supports effectiveness of LAM under strict criteria.

title: Heavy menstrual bleeding: assessment and management (NG88)
notes: Official NICE guideline on heavy menstrual bleeding.

title: CDC - Lactational Amenorrhea Method (US MEC Appendix G)
notes: Official CDC criteria for LAM use.

title: WHO - Restricting caffeine intake during pregnancy
notes: WHO recommendation regarding caffeine intake during pregnancy.

title: ACOG - Moderate Caffeine Consumption During Pregnancy
notes: ACOG guidance on moderate caffeine intake limits.

title: משרד הבריאות - חומצה פולית לפני הריון
notes: Israeli Ministry of Health recommendation on folic acid.

title: Gov.il - הריון בסיכון גבוה
notes: Official Israeli government page regarding high-risk pregnancy.
